metadata.title:
Pinchot Stands Squarely
for the Roosevelt


Policies.

pages.raw_text:
II stand for the Roosevelt
policies most of all because in them
the plain American always and ev-
erywhere holds first place. And I
propose to stand for them while
l have the strength to stand for
anything"


With these ringing words, Gift
ford Pinchot, chief forester of the
United States, who has been at
tackled by Secretary of the Interior
Ballinger as trying to run the in
terror department" today took up
the gauntlet of war laid down by
Ballinger in an address before the
Seventeenth National Irrigation
congress.


When Pinchot took his place on
the platform this morning the con-
vention leaped upon chairs and
went wild with enthusiasm, re-
flecting faithfully the sentiment of
the whole nation against the land
